---
title: "CompatibilizaĂ§ĂŁo de Setores CensitĂˇrios 2000â†”2010 via Grafos"
subtitle: "ExploraĂ§ĂŁo dos resultados do CompatMalhas para RO, AL e SC"
date: "2026-04-13"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "CĂłdigo"
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 7
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
knitr:
  opts_knit:
    root.dir: "d:/Dropbox/Artigos/geobr_prep_data"
---

```{r setup}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(leaflet)
library(mapview)
library(arrow)
library(jsonlite)
library(knitr)

sf_use_s2(FALSE)
options(scipen = 999)
mapviewOptions(fgb = FALSE)

# Paths
dir_compat <- "data-raw/census_tract/compat"
dir_data   <- "data/census_tract"
ufs <- c("11" = "RondĂ´nia", "27" = "Alagoas", "42" = "Santa Catarina")

# Helper: expand range notation
expand_range <- function(geocodigo) {
  parts <- strsplit(geocodigo, "-")[[1]]
  if (length(parts) < 2) return(geocodigo)
  prefix <- parts[1]; suffix <- parts[2]
  nd <- nchar(suffix)
  base <- substr(prefix, 1, nchar(prefix) - nd)
  s <- as.integer(substr(prefix, nchar(prefix) - nd + 1, nchar(prefix)))
  e <- as.integer(suffix)
  sprintf(paste0(base, "%0", nd, "d"), s:e)
}

# Helper: read geoarrow parquet
read_gpq <- function(path) {
  df <- arrow::read_parquet(path)
  sfc <- sf::st_as_sfc(df$geometry)
  sf::st_crs(sfc) <- 4674
  df$geometry <- NULL
  sf::st_sf(df, geometry = sfc)
}
```

# 1. IntroduĂ§ĂŁo

## O problema da range notation

O shapefile rural do IBGE para o Censo 2000 foi publicado na escala 1:500.000,
muito inferior Ă  escala urbana (~1:5.000). Nessa escala, o IBGE agregou setores
censitĂˇrios contĂ­guos em polĂ­gonos Ăşnicos, usando uma notaĂ§ĂŁo de "range" no
campo `geocodigo`:

> `120033605000001-0003` = setores 001, 002 e 003 compartilham o mesmo polĂ­gono

Isso resulta em **22.431 setores** (de 215.811 no censobr) sem geometria
individual â€” 10,4% do total.

## O algoritmo CompatMalhas

Aplicamos o algoritmo CompatMalhas (MendonĂ§a & Kon, COURB 2025) para
construir um **grafo bipartido** de correspondĂŞncia entre os setores de
2000 e 2010. O algoritmo opera em 3 etapas:

1. **ManutenĂ§ĂŁo**: setores com mesmo ID e localizaĂ§ĂŁo preservada (1:1)
2. **DivisĂŁo**: setores onde â‰Ą70% da Ăˇrea se sobrepĂµe (1:N)
3. **SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo forĂ§ada**: buffer negativo (-50m, -20m, 0m) para setores isolados

## Estados de teste

| UF | Estado | Motivo da escolha |
|----|--------|-------------------|
| 11 | RondĂ´nia | Pequeno, poucas ranges (94), rĂˇpido |
| 27 | Alagoas | MĂ©dio, muitas ranges (450), 78 componentes multi-range |
| 42 | Santa Catarina | Grande, 601 ranges, range mĂˇx. 355 setores |

## ParĂ˘metros utilizados

| ParĂ˘metro | Valor | PadrĂŁo CompatMalhas | Justificativa |
|-----------|-------|---------------------|---------------|
| A/P ratio | 0.2 | 0.3 | PolĂ­gonos 1:500k geram slivers maiores |
| L_min | 0.7 | 0.8 | Baixa resoluĂ§ĂŁo â†’ menos overlap puro |
| Buffers | -50, -20, 0 | -20, -10, 0 | ImprecisĂŁo de bordas ~100-500m |

# 2. VisĂŁo geral dos resultados

```{r load-reports}
#| label: load-reports

reports <- lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  fromJSON(file.path(dir_compat, paste0("compat_", uf, "_report.json")))
})
names(reports) <- names(ufs)

# Build summary table
summary_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  r <- reports[[uf]]
  data.frame(
    UF = paste0(uf, " (", ufs[uf], ")"),
    Setores_2000 = r$layers$`Setores C1`,
    Setores_2010 = r$layers$`Setores C2`,
    Correspondencias = r$relationships$`Total relationships`$n,
    Manutencao = r$relationships$`ManutenĂ§ĂŁo`$n,
    Divisao = r$relationships$`DivisĂŁo`$n,
    Sobreposicao = r$relationships$`SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-50m)`$n +
                   r$relationships$`SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-20m)`$n +
                   r$relationships$`SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (0m)`$n,
    AMCs = r$operations$`Total redesign operations`$n,
    AMC_1to1 = r$operations$`1:1`$n,
    AMC_1toN = r$operations$`1:n`$n,
    AMC_MtoN = r$operations$`m:n`$n,
    Desconexos = r$metrics$`Unconnected polygons`$n,
    Isolados_persist = r$metrics$`Persistent unconnected`$n,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
```

## Tabela resumo

```{r summary-table}
#| label: summary-table
kable(summary_df, col.names = c(
  "UF", "Setores 2000", "Setores 2010", "CorrespondĂŞncias",
  "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo", "DivisĂŁo", "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo",
  "AMCs", "1:1", "1:N", "m:N",
  "Desconexos", "Isolados persist."
), caption = "Resultados do CompatMalhas por UF")
```

## DistribuiĂ§ĂŁo de correspondĂŞncias por mĂ©todo

```{r bar-relationships}
#| label: bar-relationships
#| fig-cap: "DistribuiĂ§ĂŁo de correspondĂŞncias por mĂ©todo e UF"

rel_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  r <- reports[[uf]]$relationships
  data.frame(
    UF = ufs[uf],
    Metodo = c("ManutenĂ§ĂŁo", "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo desassoc.",
               "DivisĂŁo", "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-50m)",
               "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-20m)", "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (0m)"),
    N = c(r$`ManutenĂ§ĂŁo`$n, r$`ManutenĂ§ĂŁo desassociada`$n,
          r$`DivisĂŁo`$n, r$`SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-50m)`$n,
          r$`SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-20m)`$n, r$`SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (0m)`$n),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

rel_df$Metodo <- factor(rel_df$Metodo, levels = rev(c(
  "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo", "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo desassoc.", "DivisĂŁo",
  "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-50m)", "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-20m)", "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (0m)"
)))

ggplot(rel_df, aes(x = UF, y = N, fill = Metodo)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo" = "#2ecc71",
    "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo desassoc." = "#27ae60",
    "DivisĂŁo" = "#3498db",
    "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-50m)" = "#e74c3c",
    "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (-20m)" = "#e67e22",
    "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo (0m)" = "#f39c12"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(y = "ProporĂ§ĂŁo", fill = "MĂ©todo") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_flip()
```

**ObservaĂ§ĂŁo**: A sobreposiĂ§ĂŁo forĂ§ada domina (53-76%) porque os polĂ­gonos
2000 de baixa resoluĂ§ĂŁo (1:500k) raramente coincidem geometricamente com os
setores 2010. O `findMaintenances()` encontra poucos matches por ID porque
os setores com range notation tĂŞm IDs diferentes dos de 2010.

## DistribuiĂ§ĂŁo de operaĂ§Ăµes de redesenho (AMCs)

```{r bar-operations}
#| label: bar-operations
#| fig-cap: "Tipos de AMC (operaĂ§ĂŁo de redesenho) por UF"

ops_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  r <- reports[[uf]]$operations
  data.frame(
    UF = ufs[uf],
    Tipo = c("1:1", "1:N", "N:1", "m:N"),
    N = c(r$`1:1`$n, r$`1:n`$n, r$`n:1`$n, r$`m:n`$n),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

ops_df$Tipo <- factor(ops_df$Tipo, levels = c("1:1", "1:N", "N:1", "m:N"))

ggplot(ops_df, aes(x = UF, y = N, fill = Tipo)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1:1"="#2ecc71", "1:N"="#3498db",
                                "N:1"="#e67e22", "m:N"="#e74c3c")) +
  labs(y = "NĂşmero de AMCs", fill = "Tipo") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_flip()
```

# 3. Anatomia dos componentes com range notation

```{r load-compat-data}
#| label: load-compat-data

# Load compat CSVs and geometries for all 3 UFs
compat_data <- lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  c2000 <- read.delim(file.path(dir_compat, paste0("compat_", uf, "_C2000_", uf, ".csv")))
  c2010 <- read.delim(file.path(dir_compat, paste0("compat_", uf, "_C2010_", uf, ".csv")))
  geom_2000 <- sf::st_read(file.path(dir_compat, paste0("setores_2000_", uf, ".gpkg")),
                            quiet = TRUE)
  geom_2010 <- sf::st_read(file.path(dir_compat, paste0("setores_2010_", uf, ".gpkg")),
                            quiet = TRUE)

  # Identify ranges
  c2000$is_range <- grepl("-", c2000$ID)
  c2000$n_expanded <- sapply(c2000$ID, function(id) length(expand_range(id)))

  list(c2000 = c2000, c2010 = c2010, geom_2000 = geom_2000, geom_2010 = geom_2010)
})
names(compat_data) <- names(ufs)
```

## VisĂŁo geral das ranges por UF

```{r range-summary}
#| label: range-summary

range_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  d <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2000
  ranges <- d[d$is_range, ]

  data.frame(
    UF = paste0(uf, " (", ufs[uf], ")"),
    Total_IDs_2000 = nrow(d),
    Ranges = sum(d$is_range),
    Degeneradas = sum(ranges$n_expanded == 1),
    Pct_degeneradas = round(100 * sum(ranges$n_expanded == 1) / max(nrow(ranges), 1), 1),
    Codigos_expandidos = sum(ranges$n_expanded),
    Range_max = max(ranges$n_expanded),
    Range_mediana = median(ranges$n_expanded),
    MCAs_com_range = length(unique(ranges$CD_MCA)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

kable(range_summary, col.names = c(
  "UF", "IDs 2000", "Ranges", "Degeneradas (X-X)", "% degen.",
  "CĂłdigos expandidos", "Maior range", "Mediana", "MCAs c/ range"
), caption = "CaracterizaĂ§ĂŁo das ranges por UF")
```

## DistribuiĂ§ĂŁo de tamanho das ranges

```{r range-histogram}
#| label: range-histogram
#| fig-cap: "DistribuiĂ§ĂŁo do tamanho das ranges (N setores por range)"

range_sizes <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  d <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2000
  ranges <- d[d$is_range & d$n_expanded > 1, ]
  data.frame(UF = ufs[uf], N = ranges$n_expanded)
}))

ggplot(range_sizes, aes(x = N)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "#3498db", alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~UF, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "N setores por range (escala log)", y = "FrequĂŞncia") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

## Componentes com mĂşltiplas ranges

```{r multi-range}
#| label: multi-range

multi_range <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  d <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2000
  ranges <- d[d$is_range, ]
  mca_counts <- table(ranges$CD_MCA)
  multi <- mca_counts[mca_counts > 1]
  if (length(multi) == 0) return(NULL)

  data.frame(
    UF = paste0(uf, " (", ufs[uf], ")"),
    N_MCAs_multi_range = length(multi),
    Max_ranges_per_MCA = max(multi),
    Total_ranges_in_multi = sum(multi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

if (!is.null(multi_range)) {
  kable(multi_range, caption = "Componentes com mĂşltiplas ranges por UF")
} else {
  cat("Nenhum componente com mĂşltiplas ranges encontrado.")
}
```

## ClassificaĂ§ĂŁo: match exato, parcial, zero

```{r range-match}
#| label: range-match

match_class <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  c2000 <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2000
  c2010 <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2010
  ranges <- c2000[c2000$is_range, ]

  ids_2010 <- c2010$ID

  result <- sapply(seq_len(nrow(ranges)), function(i) {
    expanded <- expand_range(ranges$ID[i])
    mca <- ranges$CD_MCA[i]
    ids_2010_mca <- c2010$ID[c2010$CD_MCA == mca]
    n_match <- sum(expanded %in% ids_2010_mca)
    if (n_match == length(expanded)) "EXACT"
    else if (n_match > 0) "PARTIAL"
    else "ZERO"
  })

  data.frame(
    UF = paste0(uf, " (", ufs[uf], ")"),
    EXACT = sum(result == "EXACT"),
    PARTIAL = sum(result == "PARTIAL"),
    ZERO = sum(result == "ZERO"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

kable(match_class, caption = "ClassificaĂ§ĂŁo das ranges por tipo de match com 2010")
```

# 4. Mapas interativos: anatomia de componentes

```{r select-components}
#| label: select-components

# For RO (small), select interesting components
uf_ex <- "11"
c2000_ex <- compat_data[[uf_ex]]$c2000
c2010_ex <- compat_data[[uf_ex]]$c2010
geom_2000_ex <- compat_data[[uf_ex]]$geom_2000
geom_2010_ex <- compat_data[[uf_ex]]$geom_2010

# Find a 1:1 component (1 id each side)
mca_counts_2000 <- table(c2000_ex$CD_MCA)
mca_counts_2010 <- table(c2010_ex$CD_MCA)
mcas_1to1 <- names(mca_counts_2000)[mca_counts_2000 == 1 &
              names(mca_counts_2000) %in% names(mca_counts_2010[mca_counts_2010 == 1])]

# Find a component with range (1:N)
ranges_ex <- c2000_ex[c2000_ex$is_range & c2000_ex$n_expanded > 3, ]
mcas_range <- unique(ranges_ex$CD_MCA)[1:3]

# Find a m:n component
mca_complex <- names(mca_counts_2000)[mca_counts_2000 > 3]
mca_complex <- mca_complex[mca_complex %in% names(mca_counts_2010[mca_counts_2010 > 5])][1]
```

## Mapa: Componente 1:1 (manutenĂ§ĂŁo)

```{r map-1to1}
#| label: map-1to1

# Find a 1:1 MCA where both sides have geometry in GPKG
found_1to1 <- FALSE
for (mca_id in mcas_1to1) {
  ids_2000 <- c2000_ex$ID[c2000_ex$CD_MCA == mca_id]
  ids_2010 <- c2010_ex$ID[c2010_ex$CD_MCA == mca_id]
  g2000 <- geom_2000_ex[geom_2000_ex$ID %in% ids_2000, ]
  g2010 <- geom_2010_ex[geom_2010_ex$ID %in% ids_2010, ]
  if (nrow(g2000) > 0 && nrow(g2010) > 0) {
    found_1to1 <- TRUE; break
  }
}

if (found_1to1) {
  m <- mapview(g2000, col.regions = "#e74c3c", alpha.regions = 0.3,
               layer.name = "2000", label = g2000$ID) +
       mapview(g2010, col.regions = "#3498db", alpha.regions = 0.3,
               layer.name = "2010", label = g2010$ID)
  m
} else {
  cat("Nenhum componente 1:1 com geometria em ambos os lados encontrado.")
}
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Vermelho** = polĂ­gono 2000. **Azul** = setor 2010. Em componentes 1:1,
os dois polĂ­gonos devem sobrepor-se quase perfeitamente.
:::

## Mapa: Componente com range notation (1:N ou m:N)

```{r map-range}
#| label: map-range

found_range <- FALSE
for (mca_id in na.omit(mcas_range)) {
  ids_2000 <- c2000_ex$ID[c2000_ex$CD_MCA == mca_id]
  ids_2010 <- c2010_ex$ID[c2010_ex$CD_MCA == mca_id]
  g2000 <- geom_2000_ex[geom_2000_ex$ID %in% ids_2000, ]
  g2010 <- geom_2010_ex[geom_2010_ex$ID %in% ids_2010, ]
  if (nrow(g2000) > 0 && nrow(g2010) > 0) { found_range <- TRUE; break }
}

if (found_range) {
  g2000$tipo <- ifelse(grepl("-", g2000$ID), "range", "individual")
  layers <- list()
  if (any(g2000$tipo == "range")) {
    layers[[1]] <- mapview(g2000[g2000$tipo == "range", ], col.regions = "#e74c3c",
                 alpha.regions = 0.2, layer.name = "2000 (range)", lwd = 3)
  }
  if (any(g2000$tipo == "individual")) {
    layers[[length(layers)+1]] <- mapview(g2000[g2000$tipo == "individual", ],
                 col.regions = "#27ae60", alpha.regions = 0.4,
                 layer.name = "2000 (individual)")
  }
  layers[[length(layers)+1]] <- mapview(g2010, col.regions = "#3498db",
               alpha.regions = 0.3, layer.name = "2010")
  Reduce(`+`, layers)
} else {
  cat("Nenhum componente com range e geometria encontrado.")
}
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Vermelho grosso** = polĂ­gono agregado 2000 (range notation).
**Verde** = setores individuais 2000 (Ă˘ncoras).
**Azul** = setores 2010 individuais.
O objetivo Ă© "partir" o vermelho usando os azuis como guia.
:::

## Mapa: Componente m:N complexo

```{r map-complex}
#| label: map-complex

found_complex <- FALSE
if (!is.null(mca_complex) && !is.na(mca_complex)) {
  ids_2000 <- c2000_ex$ID[c2000_ex$CD_MCA == mca_complex]
  ids_2010 <- c2010_ex$ID[c2010_ex$CD_MCA == mca_complex]
  g2000 <- geom_2000_ex[geom_2000_ex$ID %in% ids_2000, ]
  g2010 <- geom_2010_ex[geom_2010_ex$ID %in% ids_2010, ]
  if (nrow(g2000) > 0 && nrow(g2010) > 0) found_complex <- TRUE
}

if (found_complex) {
  m <- mapview(g2000, col.regions = "#e74c3c", alpha.regions = 0.3,
               layer.name = paste0("2000 (", nrow(g2000), " setores)"),
               label = g2000$ID) +
       mapview(g2010, col.regions = "#3498db", alpha.regions = 0.3,
               layer.name = paste0("2010 (", nrow(g2010), " setores)"),
               label = g2010$ID)
  m
} else {
  cat("Nenhum componente m:N com geometria encontrado.")
}
```

# 5. AnĂˇlise de Ă˘ncoras e residuais

```{r anchor-analysis}
#| label: anchor-analysis

# For each UF, analyze anchor coverage in range components
anchor_stats <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  c2000 <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2000
  c2010 <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2010
  geom_2000 <- compat_data[[uf]]$geom_2000

  ranges <- c2000[c2000$is_range & c2000$n_expanded > 1, ]
  all_ids_2000 <- geom_2000$ID

  do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(ranges$CD_MCA), function(mca) {
    # IDs in this MCA
    ids_2000_mca <- c2000$ID[c2000$CD_MCA == mca]
    range_ids <- ids_2000_mca[grepl("-", ids_2000_mca)]
    normal_ids <- ids_2000_mca[!grepl("-", ids_2000_mca)]

    # Expand all ranges
    all_expanded <- unique(unlist(lapply(range_ids, expand_range)))

    # Anchors: expanded codes that have individual geometry in 2000
    anchors <- all_expanded[all_expanded %in% all_ids_2000]
    # Also add normal IDs as anchors
    anchors <- unique(c(anchors, normal_ids[normal_ids %in% all_ids_2000]))

    # Pendentes: expanded codes WITHOUT geometry
    pendentes <- all_expanded[!all_expanded %in% all_ids_2000]
    pendentes <- pendentes[!pendentes %in% normal_ids]

    n_2010 <- sum(c2010$CD_MCA == mca)

    data.frame(
      UF = ufs[uf],
      CD_MCA = mca,
      N_ranges = length(range_ids),
      N_expanded = length(all_expanded),
      N_normal = length(normal_ids),
      N_anchors = length(anchors),
      N_pendentes = length(pendentes),
      N_2010 = n_2010,
      Pct_anchored = round(100 * length(anchors) / max(length(all_expanded) + length(normal_ids), 1), 1),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
}))
```

## DistribuiĂ§ĂŁo de cobertura por Ă˘ncoras

```{r anchor-histogram}
#| label: anchor-histogram
#| fig-cap: "ProporĂ§ĂŁo de setores com geometria individual (Ă˘ncoras) por componente"

ggplot(anchor_stats, aes(x = Pct_anchored)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "#27ae60", alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~UF, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "% de setores com geometria (Ă˘ncoras)", y = "N componentes") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

## RelaĂ§ĂŁo: tamanho do componente vs cobertura

```{r anchor-scatter}
#| label: anchor-scatter
#| fig-cap: "N setores expandidos vs % com geometria individual"

ggplot(anchor_stats, aes(x = N_expanded + N_normal, y = Pct_anchored, color = UF)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "N setores no componente (log)", y = "% com geometria (Ă˘ncoras)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5)
```

## Resumo de Ă˘ncoras por UF

```{r anchor-summary-table}
#| label: anchor-summary-table

anchor_agg <- anchor_stats |>
  group_by(UF) |>
  summarise(
    Componentes = n(),
    Total_anchors = sum(N_anchors),
    Total_pendentes = sum(N_pendentes),
    Mediana_pct = median(Pct_anchored),
    Comp_100pct = sum(Pct_anchored == 100),
    Comp_0pct = sum(Pct_anchored == 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kable(anchor_agg, col.names = c(
  "UF", "Componentes c/ range", "Total Ă˘ncoras", "Total pendentes",
  "Mediana % cobert.", "100% cobert.", "0% cobert."
), caption = "Resumo da anĂˇlise de Ă˘ncoras")
```

# 6. VisualizaĂ§ĂŁo do grafo

```{r graph-viz}
#| label: graph-viz
#| fig-cap: "Grafo de compatibilidade para um componente com range (RO)"
#| fig-height: 8

library(igraph)

# Pick a medium-complexity component from RO
if (exists("mcas_range") && length(mcas_range) > 0 && !is.na(mcas_range[1])) {
  mca_id <- mcas_range[1]

  ids_2000 <- c2000_ex$ID[c2000_ex$CD_MCA == mca_id]
  ids_2010 <- c2010_ex$ID[c2010_ex$CD_MCA == mca_id]

  # Create bipartite graph
  nodes <- data.frame(
    name = c(paste0("2000:", ids_2000), paste0("2010:", ids_2010)),
    type = c(rep(FALSE, length(ids_2000)), rep(TRUE, length(ids_2010))),
    label = c(
      ifelse(grepl("-", ids_2000), paste0(ids_2000, " [R]"),
             substr(ids_2000, 12, 15)),
      substr(ids_2010, 12, 15)
    ),
    color = c(
      ifelse(grepl("-", ids_2000), "#e74c3c", "#27ae60"),
      rep("#3498db", length(ids_2010))
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Read MCA GPKG edges layer for this component
  edges_layer <- tryCatch({
    sf::st_read(file.path(dir_compat, paste0("compat_", uf_ex, "_MCA.gpkg")),
                layer = paste0("C2000_", uf_ex, "-C2010_", uf_ex, "_edges"),
                quiet = TRUE)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)

  if (!is.null(edges_layer)) {
    # Filter edges for this MCA's nodes
    edges_filt <- edges_layer[
      edges_layer$A.nome %in% ids_2000 | edges_layer$B.nome %in% ids_2010, ]

    if (nrow(edges_filt) > 0) {
      edge_df <- data.frame(
        from = paste0("2000:", edges_filt$A.nome),
        to = paste0("2010:", edges_filt$B.nome),
        metodo = sf::st_drop_geometry(edges_filt)$metodo,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )

      g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_df, directed = FALSE, vertices = nodes)

      edge_colors <- dplyr::case_when(
        E(g)$metodo == "ManutenĂ§ĂŁo" ~ "#2ecc71",
        E(g)$metodo == "DivisĂŁo" ~ "#3498db",
        grepl("SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo", E(g)$metodo) ~ "#e74c3c",
        TRUE ~ "#999999"
      )

      layout <- layout_as_bipartite(g)
      plot(g,
           layout = layout,
           vertex.color = V(g)$color,
           vertex.label = V(g)$label,
           vertex.label.cex = 0.7,
           vertex.size = 8,
           edge.color = edge_colors,
           edge.width = 2,
           main = paste("Componente", mca_id))
      legend("bottomright",
             legend = c("ManutenĂ§ĂŁo", "DivisĂŁo", "SobreposiĂ§ĂŁo",
                        "2000 range", "2000 individual", "2010"),
             col = c("#2ecc71", "#3498db", "#e74c3c",
                     "#e74c3c", "#27ae60", "#3498db"),
             pch = c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 16, 16),
             lty = c(1, 1, 1, NA, NA, NA),
             lwd = 2, cex = 0.8)
    }
  }
}
```

# 7. DiagnĂłstico de problemas e limitaĂ§Ăµes

```{r diagnostics}
#| label: diagnostics

diag_df <- data.frame(
  Edge_case = c(
    "EC-04: MĂşltiplas ranges/componente",
    "EC-13: Ranges degeneradas (X-X)",
    "EC-07: Inconsistent splittings",
    "EC-02: Isolados persistentes",
    "EC-14: Ranges sobrepostas"
  ),
  RO = c(
    sum(table(compat_data[["11"]]$c2000$CD_MCA[compat_data[["11"]]$c2000$is_range]) > 1),
    sum(compat_data[["11"]]$c2000$is_range & compat_data[["11"]]$c2000$n_expanded == 1),
    reports[["11"]]$metrics$`Inconsistent splitting`$n,
    reports[["11"]]$metrics$`Persistent unconnected`$n,
    "verificar"
  ),
  AL = c(
    sum(table(compat_data[["27"]]$c2000$CD_MCA[compat_data[["27"]]$c2000$is_range]) > 1),
    sum(compat_data[["27"]]$c2000$is_range & compat_data[["27"]]$c2000$n_expanded == 1),
    reports[["27"]]$metrics$`Inconsistent splitting`$n,
    reports[["27"]]$metrics$`Persistent unconnected`$n,
    "verificar"
  ),
  SC = c(
    sum(table(compat_data[["42"]]$c2000$CD_MCA[compat_data[["42"]]$c2000$is_range]) > 1),
    sum(compat_data[["42"]]$c2000$is_range & compat_data[["42"]]$c2000$n_expanded == 1),
    reports[["42"]]$metrics$`Inconsistent splitting`$n,
    reports[["42"]]$metrics$`Persistent unconnected`$n,
    "verificar"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(diag_df, caption = "Edge cases encontrados por UF")
```

## Mapa: Setores isolados persistentes (RO)

```{r map-isolated}
#| label: map-isolated

# Isolated = sectors not in any MCA
all_in_mca_2000 <- compat_data[["11"]]$c2000$ID
all_geom_2000 <- compat_data[["11"]]$geom_2000
isolated_2000 <- all_geom_2000[!all_geom_2000$ID %in% all_in_mca_2000, ]

if (nrow(isolated_2000) > 0) {
  mapview(isolated_2000, col.regions = "#e74c3c",
          layer.name = "Isolados 2000", label = isolated_2000$ID)
} else {
  cat("Nenhum setor 2000 isolado (todos estĂŁo em alguma MCA).")
}
```

# 8. ComparaĂ§ĂŁo antes/depois (estimativa)

```{r before-after}
#| label: before-after

# Estimate: how many tracts would we gain?
ba_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ufs), function(uf) {
  c2000 <- compat_data[[uf]]$c2000

  # Current unified: non-range IDs from 2000
  current <- sum(!c2000$is_range)

  # After expansion: all expanded codes (deduped)
  all_expanded <- unique(unlist(lapply(c2000$ID[c2000$is_range], expand_range)))
  all_normal <- c2000$ID[!c2000$is_range]
  after <- length(unique(c(all_normal, all_expanded)))

  # Gain
  gained <- after - current

  data.frame(
    UF = paste0(uf, " (", ufs[uf], ")"),
    Atual = current,
    Estimado_apos = after,
    Ganho = gained,
    Pct_ganho = round(100 * gained / current, 1),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

kable(ba_df, col.names = c("UF", "Setores atual", "Estimado apĂłs", "Ganho", "% ganho"),
      caption = "Estimativa de ganho com desmembramento")
```

# 9. Performance

```{r performance}
#| label: performance

perf_df <- data.frame(
  UF = paste(names(ufs), "(", ufs, ")"),
  Setores_total = sapply(names(ufs), function(uf)
    reports[[uf]]$layers$`Setores C1` + reports[[uf]]$layers$`Setores C2`),
  Tempo_python = c("~20s", "~27s", "~2min"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

kable(perf_df, col.names = c("UF", "Setores (2000+2010)", "Tempo Python"),
      caption = "Tempos de execuĂ§ĂŁo do CompatMalhas")
```

**ExtrapolaĂ§ĂŁo para 27 UFs**: ~2-2.5h sequencial. SP (114k setores) domina
com ~40 min. Com 4 workers paralelos: ~50-70 min.

# 10. DecisĂµes e prĂłximos passos

## DecisĂµes tomadas

1. **Usar CompatMalhas como-estĂˇ** (nĂŁo reimplementar em R)
2. **Python prĂ©-computado** fora do pipeline targets
3. **1 polĂ­gono por range** no grafo (nĂŁo expandir antes)
4. **ParĂ˘metros adaptados**: A/P=0.2, L_min=0.7, buffers maiores
5. **EstratĂ©gia de Ă˘ncoras+residual** para desmembrar componentes mistos

## LimitaĂ§Ăµes aceitas

- Componentes rurais grandes: Ă˘ncoras cobrem <6%, estratĂ©gia degenera
- Ranges sem match em 2010 (~12%): mantidas como rural_aggregated
- Inconsistent splittings (18-71%): requer verificaĂ§ĂŁo visual
- Tempo total para 27 UFs: ~2-3h (aceitĂˇvel como prĂ©-computaĂ§ĂŁo Ăşnica)

## PrĂłximos passos

1. Rodar CompatMalhas para as 24 UFs restantes
2. Implementar `read_compat_graph()`, `build_compat_components()`,
   `apply_compat_partition()` em R
3. Integrar no `clean_censustract_2000()`
4. Validar com tar_make para 3 UFs â†’ depois 27 UFs
5. Atualizar BACKLOG.md e MEMORY.md
